item.title: Tech Selloff, Bitcoin Drop Test Retail Investor Strength Ahead of SpaceX IPO
item.description: For years, Wall Street has benefited from one of the most reliable forces in modern markets: a retail-trader army willing to buy almost anything.

item.title: Marvell Technology, Flex to Join S&P 500 Later This Month
item.description: Marvell Technology Inc. and Flex Ltd. will join the S&P 500 in the latest quarterly rebalance, S&P Dow Jones Indices said Friday.

item.title: Goldman’s Flood Sees Buying Opportunity in Stock Market Selloff
item.description: Friday’s pullback in US equities offers a chance to add exposure rather than a reason to retreat, with a clear path for the S&P 500 to reach 8,000 this year, according to John Flood, the head of Americas equities execution services at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Apollo Wraps Up $35 Billion Debt to Buy AI Chips for Anthropic
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. and Blackstone Inc. have finalized a $35 billion financing package for Anthropic PBC to expand its AI infrastructure, marking the latest mega-deal in the artificial intelligence race.

item.title: Google-Tied Prepaid Energy Bonds See Flood of Muni Trader Demand
item.description: Google parent Alphabet Inc.’s municipal-bond market debut was met by a surge of investor interest.

item.title: SpaceX and Other Mega IPOs May Wait Years to Join the S&P 500
item.description: Mega-IPO candidates including SpaceX are expected to face a long road to entry to the S&P 500 Index, after the company that makes the rules rejected a proposal that included relaxing the requirement that they be profitable.

item.title: Tech Leaders Mull the Next Phase of AI as Top Labs Plan IPOs
item.description: Executives and academics discuss the new unknowns emerging for AI

item.title: The Knicks Economy and the Actual Economy
item.description: Some thoughts on the NBA finals and inflation.

item.title: Citigroup Economists Maintain Lonely Call for Fed Rate Cuts
item.description: Unswayed by strong US jobs data released Friday, economists at Citigroup Inc. are maintaining their increasingly lonely prediction that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates three times this year.

item.title: New York Summer Camp Charging $16,750 Files for Bankruptcy
item.description: Simad Holdings, the operator of youth sleep-away camps in the Northeast including a popular retreat in New York’s Catskill mountains charging $16,750 for the summer, has filed for bankruptcy.

item.title: Wall Street Sours on Lululemon Shares After Sales Outlook Cut
item.description: Only one Wall Street analyst still rates Lululemon Athletica Inc. as a buy after the upscale yogawear company cut its annual forecast amid slumping sales in North America.

item.title: Will JPMorgan Buy Dimensional? An ETF Newsletter Can Dream
item.description: Mapping out what a mega active ETF merger might look like

item.title: US Consumer Borrowing Posts Biggest Back-to-Back Gain Since 2022
item.description: US consumer borrowing posted another strong increase in April, in the biggest back-to-back gain since the end of 2022.

item.title: Google Buying Computing From SpaceX in $920-Million-a-Month Deal
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google has agreed to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for computing power as part of a cloud-services deal that runs through mid-2029, according to a company filing.

item.title: S&P’s SpaceX Snub Shows Elon Musk the Power of Index Gatekeepers
item.description: Elon Musk once said passive investing has “gone too far,” mocked environmental, social and governance indexes as a “scam” and endured a long wait to get Tesla Inc. into the S&P 500.

item.title: Gold Erases This Year’s Gains as Jobs Data Fuel Fed-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold erased its gains this year as robust US jobs data fueled bets that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year, a headwind for the precious metal.

item.title: Meta Considers Raising Billions in Share Sale, FT Reports
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is considering raising equity after Alphabet Inc.’s successful $85 billion sale, according to the Financial Times. The shares fell on the report.

item.title: Revolut Weighs Secondary Share Sale at $115 Billion Valuation
item.description: Revolut Ltd. is looking to run a secondary share sale that would value the digital bank at $115 billion, on the heels of receiving a UK bank license and applying for a charter in the US, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Why Ireland Distorts the Euro Zone’s GDP Numbers
item.description: Few countries punch above their weight as much as Ireland does. Culturally, the tiny island nation of just 5 million people boasts award-winning actors such as Cillian Murphy, world renown brands like Guinness, and some of the best butter anywhere. Ireland’s leader is practically guaranteed a White House meeting with the US president every year on St. Patrick’s Day.

item.title: Canada’s TerraVest Slides as Chairman Accused of Insider Tipping
item.description: Shares of Canadian manufacturer TerraVest Industries Inc. fell the most since 2009 over allegations of insider tipping by its executive chairman.

item.title: ‘Grand Theft Auto VI’ Scares Away All the Other Video Games
item.description: September has become the most crowded release month of the year as publishers steer clear of Rockstar’s game

item.title: Data Centers Have a New Adversary: Tigers and Leopards at a Zoo
item.description: A proposal for a data center in Nashville is facing off against the leopards and tigers of the city’s zoo.

item.title: Warsh Faces Early Test as Blowout Jobs Fuel Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: The US labor market is back on its feet, strong employment data showed Friday, stoking concerns of another inflation sting and bolstering arguments from some Federal Reserve officials that they may need to raise interest rates later this year.

item.title: World Cup Likely Gave a Lift to Blowout US, Canada Jobs Numbers
item.description: Strong hiring in leisure and hospitality helped fuel a surge in hiring in the US and Canada last month as the two nations geared up to host the FIFA World Cup starting next week.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Remains Near Zero as Peace Talks Stall
item.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was limited to just a few ships over the past 24 hours as peace talks between the US and Iran show little sign of advancing.

item.title: Fitch Lifts South Africa Rating on Fiscal Progress, Management
item.description: Fitch Ratings raised South Africa’s credit rating, citing the nation’s progress in tightening finances.

item.title: US, Mexico, Canada to Miss July USMCA Date, Ramping Up Trade Tension
item.description: The US, Mexico and Canada are set to blow past a July 1 milestone to renew their trade deal, opening the possibility of months or years of haggling over the rules and tariffs for auto manufacturing and other industries, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: OpenAI Would’ve ‘Imploded’ If Altman Didn’t Return, Ex-CTO Says
item.description: OpenAI would likely have unraveled if Sam Altman hadn’t returned as chief executive officer following his brief ouster in 2023, former tech chief Mira Murati said, offering her clearest account yet of one of Silicon Valley’s most dramatic boardroom battles.

item.title: TAG Immobilien Is Said to Eye Polish IPO Kickoff in Coming Weeks
item.description: German real estate group TAG Immobilien AG is considering starting the formal process for a potential initial public offering of its Polish unit Robyg SA in Warsaw as soon as the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: LinkedIn Co-Founder Reid Hoffman to Leave Microsoft’s Board
item.description: Reid Hoffman, the LinkedIn co-founder and venture capitalist, will leave Microsoft Corp.’s board later this year, saying he wants to focus on his new artificial intelligence company.
